description: A horizontal (landscape) designed announcement card in the DHS brand style on a white background. A blue header bar across the top contains the centered white spaced-out word 'ANNOUNCEMENT,' with the circular DHS seal at the left edge; a thin red rule runs beneath the header. The left portion carries a large bold navy serif headline 'Temporary Protected Status.' The right portion shows a light gray world map graphic with a red-outlined label box reading 'Haiti' and a small red star marking Haiti's location in the Caribbean, beside body text: 'Secretary Alejandro N. Mayorkas announces the extension and redesignation of Haiti for Temporary Protected Status (TPS)' (the redesignation phrase emphasized in red) 'for 18 months, from August 4, 2024, through February 3, 2026.' The style is clean, official, government-communication layout (seal, two-tone typography, map locator). Note this is a Biden-era DHS post (Secretary Mayorkas) announcing a TPS extension for Haiti, an informational/policy announcement rather than enforcement propaganda. Target audience: Haitian nationals in the U.S., advocates, and the public.
notable_text: ANNOUNCEMENT / Temporary Protected Status / Haiti / Secretary Alejandro N. Mayorkas announces the extension and redesignation of Haiti for Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for 18 months, from August 4, 2024, through February 3, 2026.
